w:r.w:t: A global hybrid publishing house committed to intellectual rigor, cultural integrity, and disciplined storytelling rooted in the African world and its diaspora.
We publish work that withstands scrutiny. We build infrastructure where narrative meets ownership.
Sankofa Publishers is a New Mexico based hybrid press built on more than 47 years of combined publishing experience across editorial, production, design, and print.
We are not a vanity press.
We publish across genres. Fiction. Non fiction. Scholarship. Cultural analysis. Memoir. Investigative work. Literary writing. Controversial but disciplined work.
SECTION 2: Why Our Publishing Is Free
Publishing should not be gated by financial barriers.
If a manuscript meets our standards and is publication ready, we publish it at no cost.
We receive only limited non exclusive distribution permission through formal agreement.
Authors may voluntarily contribute a portion of their royalties to sustain the press. This is optional and does not influence our level of commitment.
Our service is free because access to infrastructure should not be restricted by capital.
• Ebook production and global retail placement
We do not hold your rights.
We do not trap your book.
We do not restrict your growth.
It was mapped, traded with, and intellectually active while parts of Europe were still tribal.
The transatlantic slave trade was not only physical theft. It was a targeted removal of engineers, farmers, strategists, and genetic resilience.
The richest natural continent on Earth remains economically constrained not because of absence, but because of extraction.
You cannot build a sovereign future while seeking validation from systems that destabilized your past.
We publish work that understands this.
Colonization did not begin with chains. It began with distortion.
Skin bleaching, self rejection, and internalized inferiority are not preferences. They are residues.
Development without control is rebranded colonization.
Economic circulation within the diaspora strengthens independence.
We are building record, not reaction.
If your manuscript is prepared, disciplined, and aligned with intellectual responsibility, we invite you to submit.
Up to 45 day structured review process.
Clear acceptance, rejection, or conditional acceptance decisions.
We are for work that intends to endure.
Sankofa Publishers is a New Mexico based hybrid publishing house built on professional discipline and cultural responsibility.

Though the imprint itself is newly established, our team brings more than 47 years of combined experience across editorial development, manuscript evaluation, design, production management, print systems, and distribution strategy.
We understand ownership as non negotiable.
We are building a press designed to endure.
Sankofa speaks to retrieval. To go back and bring forward what carries value.
For generations, African and diasporic narratives have been filtered, diluted, reshaped, or dismissed to accommodate external expectations.
Colonization did not begin with chains. It began with distortion.
Africa was not discovered. It was mapped, traded with, and intellectually active while parts of Europe were still tribal.
The diaspora is not scattered weakness. It is dispersed continuity.
Sankofa exists to provide disciplined publishing infrastructure for work that understands this reality and approaches it with seriousness.
For most of my life, I have lived inside rooms where I was expected to shrink.

I have experienced exclusion that was never formally announced but clearly enforced. I have been overlooked in spaces where my competence was visible. I have felt the quiet pressure to soften my voice, to dilute my identity, to make others comfortable by minimizing myself.
Many in our diaspora know that feeling.
The meeting where you are qualified but not affirmed.
The platform where you are present but not centered.
The moment you are praised for being “articulate” as if intelligence was unexpected.
The subtle suggestion that loving your own people too openly means you must dislike someone else.
For years, I internalized that tension.
I hesitated to speak boldly about my African identity. I thought pride required apology. I thought affirmation of my people required explanation.
There comes a point where you realize the discomfort was never about your love. It was about your awakening.
Other communities invest in their own without hesitation. They circulate resources. They preserve narrative. They build institutions. They protect their voice.
Somewhere along the way, many of us were taught that doing the same for ourselves required permission.

When I stopped seeking validation from systems that never intended to validate me fully, something shifted. The approval I chased was never external. It was internal alignment. It was clarity. It was dignity.
Sankofa was born from that clarity.
This is not hatred for others. It is not hostility. It is not exclusion.
It is the belief that our stories deserve infrastructure.
That our intellectual work deserves discipline.
That our economic circulation deserves structure.
That our people deserve institutions that do not hesitate to say we matter.
Unity across humanity is a worthy goal. But unity without self knowledge is fragile.
We cannot become one while we remain divided within ourselves.
Sankofa exists so that those who carry insight, critique, research, imagination, and truth can document it without shrinking.
If you have ever felt the pressure to minimize your voice, this press exists so you do not have to.
The richest natural continent on Earth remains economically constrained not because of lack of resources, but because of extraction systems.
Development without control becomes rebranded colonization.
We do not hold ownership of your book.
We welcome disciplined work across genres, including:
We require clarity, preparation, and accountability.
We do not publish faith based doctrine or religious advocacy.
We are open to strong arguments.
We are not open to reckless thinking.
We do not rush unfinished manuscripts to market.
We do not publish content designed only to provoke without substance.
We do not participate in ideological extremism.
We do not seize author rights.
We do not impose forced royalty structures.
We do not dilute cultural seriousness for market trend.
We do not apologize for disciplined love of African identity.
The manuscript meets our standards and proceeds to agreement.
The manuscript does not meet alignment or structural requirements.
The manuscript shows strong potential but requires specific editorial clarification or revision before publication.
Our review window extends up to 45 days.
We communicate clearly. We decide definitively.
We were whole before we were scattered.
The diaspora is not a wound. It is continuity.
Sankofa Publishers exists for work that intends to endure beyond market cycles and beyond distortion.
If your manuscript is disciplined and your purpose is serious, we are prepared to build it properly.
Sankofa Publishers operates on a standards based hybrid publishing model.
If your manuscript meets our editorial and structural requirements and is publication ready, we publish it at no cost.
We remove financial barriers while maintaining professional publishing infrastructure.
Publishing should not be limited to those with disposable capital.
Free does not mean lower standards.
• Ebook production and international retail placement
Sankofa Publishers does not hold or retain author royalties.
Royalty payments are issued directly to authors through the Sankofa Author Portal.
• Access transparent breakdown of revenue and deductions
Royalty is calculated as 100 percent of net revenue.
Net revenue is defined as gross retail sales minus:
Authors are not required to pay for books prior to sale.
Books are printed upon order. Once sold, the print cost and associated distribution fees are deducted from the sale price before net royalty is calculated.
Authors are notified of all transactions and have full visibility into reporting.
Sankofa works collaboratively with authors to determine retail pricing.
We provide strategic pricing recommendations designed to:
Final pricing decisions are made with transparency and author involvement.
We operate with full reporting clarity.
Our systems are structured to show:
Transparency is not optional. It is structural.
Our publishing decisions are never influenced by service purchases.
Editorial acceptance is based solely on manuscript quality and alignment.
The manuscript proceeds directly to agreement and production.
The manuscript does not meet our standards or alignment criteria.
The manuscript shows strong potential but requires specified revisions, clarification, or compliance adjustments before publication proceeds.
If you choose not to use our editing services, we may recommend a paid Publishing Readiness Assessment.
Readiness protects both author and press.
Authors retain full ownership of their work at all times.
Should an author wish to discontinue publishing with Sankofa, a 30 day written notice is required to allow proper distribution withdrawal and record management according to industry standards.
We prioritize professional exit procedures that protect the author’s intellectual property.
The richest natural continent on Earth remains economically constrained not because of absence of resources, but because of extraction.
Publishing infrastructure is part of economic circulation.
We do not charge mandatory publishing fees.
We do not guarantee publication in exchange for payment.
We do not accept manuscripts based on service purchases.
Editorial acceptance is based solely on quality, preparation, and alignment with our standards.
Optional services are available, but they do not influence publishing decisions.
Because access to infrastructure should not be restricted by capital.
Too many capable voices are blocked by upfront costs.
We remove that barrier while maintaining professional standards.
Free does not mean lower quality.
Our sustainability does not depend on seizing author rights or withholding royalties.
Do you take ownership of my book?
Authors retain 100 percent copyright ownership and intellectual property control.
We receive limited non exclusive publishing and distribution permission under formal agreement.
Can only African or Black authors publish here?
Sankofa Publishers welcomes authors of all backgrounds.
However, the work must contribute value to African communities, diasporic discourse, or related intellectual and cultural conversations.
What countries can publish with Sankofa?
Authors from multiple countries may publish with us.
Royalty payouts can be issued to many international banking systems.
For countries outside our direct payment gateway reach, special arrangements can be made.
Please note: international payouts may be subject to minimal but mandatory payment processing fees outside the control of Sankofa Publishers.
Do you publish religious or faith based material?
We do not publish faith based doctrine, religious advocacy, or theological promotion.
We may consider academic, sociological, or critical examination of religion if it is structured, analytical, and non evangelical in nature.
Our focus is intellectual rigor, not religious promotion.
Are books selected based on the political or cultural views of Sankofa leadership?
No manuscript is evaluated based on whether it aligns with personal beliefs of leadership.
We do not welcome reckless argument, hate speech, or defamatory content.
Do you allow AI generated or AI assisted content?
AI assisted content is permitted if fully disclosed at submission.
Failure to disclose AI usage may result in rejection or termination of publishing agreement if discovered later.
Transparency protects both author and publisher.
Conditional Acceptance with specified revision requirements.
What if my manuscript is rejected?
Rejection simply means the manuscript does not meet current standards or alignment.
It does not mean you lack ability.
You may revise and resubmit at a later date.
Can my book be placed in physical bookstores?
We actively market titles to physical bookstores and retail buyers.
However, the decision to stock a book rests entirely with the individual bookseller.
Booksellers evaluate titles based on demand, market fit, and commercial viability.
While we cannot guarantee shelf placement, we are highly resourceful in working with retailers across multiple markets when the bookseller elects to stock the title.
Can I withdraw my book later?
Authors may withdraw their book by providing 30 days written notice.
This allows for proper discontinuation of distribution and protection of intellectual property records according to industry standards.
Publishing is free for accepted, publication ready manuscripts.
Optional services are clearly defined and separately priced.
Transparency is structural to our model.
We publish work that may critique systems, analyze power structures, or examine historical realities.
We are not a partisan organization.
We are a standards driven publishing institution.
We were whole before we were scattered.”
Sankofa Publishers serves authors at different stages of preparation.
Please select the pathway that best describes your manuscript.
For authors who believe their manuscript is fully edited, structurally sound, and production ready.
• Optionally request a Publishing Readiness Assessment
Publishing is free for manuscripts that meet our standards.
Path B: Manuscript Needs Professional Support
You may request professional services prior to submission for publication consideration.
Use this pathway if your manuscript is incomplete, unedited, or requires development.
Fiction and non fiction across genres
• Cultural, analytical, investigative, or literary work
Even experienced authors benefit from structured evaluation.
This service protects your book before distribution.
It is not mandatory for submission, but it significantly reduces the risk of conditional acceptance or production delays.
Selecting this option does not guarantee acceptance. It strengthens preparation.
All manuscripts may undergo plagiarism detection and originality screening.
• All referenced material is properly cited
Violation may result in immediate rejection or termination of agreement.
If your manuscript requires development or support, Sankofa offers professional services prior to submission.
Using our services does not guarantee publication. However, it ensures preparation aligns with our publishing standards.
You are seeking guaranteed publication without review
• You are unwilling to engage in revision if required
• You intend to submit plagiarized or undisclosed AI generated content
• You expect publication to override professional standards
We publish work that intends to endure.
We do not dilute. We refine.”
Sankofa Publishers offers professional publishing services for authors who require structural, editorial, production, or strategic support.
Using our services does not guarantee publication.
For authors submitting manuscripts they believe are publication ready.
Even strong manuscripts benefit from structured professional evaluation before entering distribution.
If you purchase the PRA at the time of submission and your manuscript is not accepted for publication, you will receive a 100 percent refund of your PRA investment.
The purpose of this service is protection, not profit.
Our editorial team brings decades of publishing experience across literary, commercial, investigative, and analytical work.
For sentence level clarity and stylistic precision.
Fully confidential. White label. Contractually protected.
Our ghostwriting team includes experienced writers who have contributed to award winning works and commercially successful titles across multiple genres.
We do not “write for hire” casually.
We build books through structured process:
• 30 percent prior to final manuscript transfer
Full payment required before final manuscript release.
Confidentiality agreements executed for all engagements.
We build books that carry weight.
Your cover determines whether your book is considered.
We design market competitive, genre aligned, strategically positioned covers.
$3,000 – $10,000 depending on length and narrator profile
Royalty share narrator options may be available depending on project viability.
For organizations, scholars, or leaders seeking:
Optional enhanced reporting and payout system.
Manual reporting every six months remains available at no cost.
We do not pressure authors into unnecessary services.
If your manuscript is ready, we will say so.
If you require support, we will recommend only what strengthens the work.
This is the exact form structure your dev can build. Includes both pathways.
Path B: Request Professional Services Consultation
PATH A FORM: Publication Ready Submission
Do you own 100% of the rights to this manuscript? (Yes/No) (required)
Does the manuscript include real people and allegations? (Yes/No) (required)
Does the manuscript include copyrighted lyrics, long quotes, or third-party media? (Yes/No) (required)
Yes, add PRA (Refundable 100% if manuscript is not accepted, excluding fraud, plagiarism, undisclosed AI)
I confirm this submission is original or properly licensed.
I confirm I hold full rights and authority to submit this work.
I confirm all AI usage has been disclosed.
I understand Sankofa may screen for plagiarism and integrity.
I understand review may take up to 45 days and outcomes include acceptance, rejection, or conditional acceptance.
I understand submission does not guarantee publication.
Display text on the form before submit:

All submitted manuscripts are treated as confidential. Sankofa Publishers adheres to U.S. copyright law and international intellectual property standards and does not claim ownership of submitted work absent a signed agreement. Submission does not transfer rights.
PATH B FORM: Professional Services Consultation
Audiobook Production (Author owns it. No royalty sharing)
Estimated Word Count or target length (required)
We are not fragments. We are foundation.”
Sankofa Publishers exists to remove financial barriers while maintaining professional publishing standards.
Editorial systems. Distribution coordination. Platform development. Compliance. Reporting. Production management.
We chose to eliminate mandatory publishing fees.
The richest natural continent on Earth remains economically constrained not because of absence, but because of extraction.
When authors voluntarily contribute a portion of their royalty or supporters give directly, they strengthen:
This is investment in narrative sovereignty.
• Allocate a voluntary portion of your royalties
Authors who choose not to contribute receive the same editorial respect and publishing commitment.
If Sankofa has strengthened your work, protected your voice, or aligned with your values, we invite you to participate in sustaining it.
The Sankofa Author Portal is designed to provide clarity, control, and visibility.
Authors should not wonder what their book is earning.
Sankofa does not retain royalty percentages.
Net revenue is defined as gross sales minus:
• Royalty payments issued every six months
• Detailed breakdown of units sold, pricing, and fees
This option is included at no cost.
For authors who prefer real time access and automated payments.
This system provides continuous visibility and streamlined payouts.
Authors who choose standard reporting receive the same royalty percentage and publishing support.
We issue royalty payouts to multiple international banking systems.
Where direct integration is unavailable, alternative arrangements can be made.
International payments may be subject to minimal gateway fees outside the control of Sankofa Publishers.
Retail pricing is determined in collaboration with the author.
We provide strategic recommendations designed to:
You are not excluded from pricing decisions.
Transparency is not marketing language. It is operational structure.
The publishing industry has historically centralized control.
We build systems that allow authors to see, understand, and control their earnings.
The Sankofa Blog exists to explore publishing, cultural memory, economic sovereignty, and intellectual responsibility.
The diaspora is not fractured weakness.
This space extends the conversation beyond the printed page.
Selected Sankofa authors may be invited to contribute structured essays to the blog.
We are not emerging. We are returning.”
Sankofa Publishers is a New Mexico based hybrid publishing house dedicated to intellectual rigor, cultural integrity, and narrative sovereignty.
We publish work that contributes to disciplined discourse across the African world and global diaspora.
For press inquiries, interview requests, or institutional collaborations, please contact:
Sankofa Publishers operates on a standards based hybrid publishing model that removes financial barriers while preserving editorial discipline.
Sankofa does not acquire author ownership.
We build record. We do not exploit it.
Sankofa was founded by a former Pastor of 33 years who transitioned from ministry into cultural infrastructure building.
The founder’s journey includes lived experience with:
Sankofa emerged from the conviction that disciplined love of African identity is not hostility toward others, but alignment with self.
Members of the press may request:
Please allow reasonable response time for interview scheduling.
Sankofa leadership is available for commentary on:
Review copies may be provided at the discretion of Sankofa for legitimate media outlets, reviewers, academic institutions, and bookstores.
Sankofa welcomes fair and responsible reporting.
Unauthorized reproduction of proprietary materials is prohibited.
Speaking inquiries may be directed to press@sankofapublishers.com
Colonization did not begin with chains. It began with distortion.
The richest natural continent on Earth remains economically constrained because extraction systems were normalized.
Publishing is not separate from economic structure.
Sankofa Publishers operates at the intersection of culture and infrastructure.
Sankofa Publishers operates as an emerging institution committed to intellectual rigor and cultural infrastructure.
We pursue strategic partnerships across publishing, academia, culture, commerce, and diaspora networks.
While specific commercial partners are not publicly disclosed, our infrastructure is built on established global publishing systems.
Institutional inquiries may be directed to:
We seek alignment with organizations focused on:
Sankofa actively markets titles to physical bookstores.
Retail stocking decisions remain at the discretion of individual booksellers.
We maintain professional outreach practices and remain resourceful in securing shelf placement where demand and alignment exist.
We collaborate with organizations seeking disciplined, professionally executed publications aligned with intellectual credibility.
We build with counsel, not impulse.”
Sankofa Publishers is guided by a multidisciplinary Advisory Board composed of leaders across publishing, academia, economics, law, and cultural strategy.
The Advisory Board provides strategic insight, ethical oversight, and institutional perspective.
Advisors do not manage daily operations but contribute to long term structural direction.
This structure ensures Sankofa grows with discipline and foresight.
Dr. Okoye specializes in diaspora economic circulation models and emerging market infrastructure. Her advisory role supports Sankofa’s long term positioning within global African economic frameworks.
Professor Laurent brings academic expertise in post colonial literary theory and global narrative structures. He advises on scholarly and analytical publishing standards.
Intellectual Property Attorney | New York, USA
Ms. Bennett provides strategic guidance on copyright protection, publishing compliance, and international intellectual property frameworks.
With over two decades in production management and distribution systems, Mr. Thompson advises on scalability and operational integrity.
Dr. Adewale focuses on cultural preservation, narrative sovereignty, and policy influence through publishing platforms.
Advisors may serve renewable terms as defined by internal governance policy.
Sankofa anticipates expanding its Advisory Board as institutional growth continues.
Prospective advisors may be invited based on demonstrated expertise and alignment with Sankofa’s mission.
Sankofa operates with defined roles, documented standards, and consistent decision pathways.
Our governance structure is designed to protect:
Sankofa is founder led and institutionally advised.
Sankofa is led by Founder and Managing Director, Mark Daniel.
Sankofa maintains an Advisory Board composed of multidisciplinary professionals.
The Advisory Board exists to strengthen:
Paid services do not influence acceptance decisions.
We do not publish unfinished work as finished product.
We welcome strong ideas and controversial arguments where:
• Intent is constructive or analytically serious
We do not publish content designed only to inflame.
Sankofa exists to support work contributing value to African communities and diasporic discourse.
We require authors to submit original work or properly licensed material.
Manuscripts may be screened for plagiarism.
If plagiarism is identified, the submission may be rejected or publication terminated.
AI assisted content is permitted only if fully disclosed.
Failure to disclose AI usage may result in:
• Termination after publication if discovered later
We do not publish defamatory or slanderous material.
Where works include real persons, events, or allegations, Sankofa may require:
Sankofa reserves the right to decline high risk works.
We do not tolerate harassment, threats, or abusive conduct.
We do not withhold royalties as leverage.
We operate with clear reporting practices and defined payment cycles.
Sankofa avoids conflicts of interest in:
Where a conflict exists, Sankofa will:
Submitted manuscripts are treated as confidential.
Ghostwriting engagements are protected by written confidentiality terms.
Sensitive author data is handled using secure operational practices and verified third party processors.
when standards, compliance, or ethical requirements are breached.
We publish with discipline because our history deserves seriousness.
We build infrastructure that outlives trend.
For general inquiries, submissions support, services, media, or partnerships, contact us below.
We respond as promptly as possible. Complex inquiries may require additional processing time.
